{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in Document [15] (Title: Physiology, Bone -- Function -- Bone Extracellular Matrix), it states that 'Inorganic bone matrix accounts for 99% of the body's storage of calcium, 85% of the phosphorus, and 40% to 60% of the magnesium and sodium.' This clearly indicates that the inorganic component of bone is 85% of phosphorus. Since phosphorus is a key component of hydroxyapatite, which is the inorganic component of bone, we can conclude that the inorganic component of bone is 85%.", "answer_choice": "C"}
